subject: [PATCH] mm/mempool: use static key for boot-time debug enablement

reviews.author: Vlastimil (SUSE)
reviews.summary:
Reviewer noted that the same issue applies to dmapool_debug, which uses __setup() instead of early_param(), and suggested considering defaulting the static key to true under CONFIG_SLUB_DEBUG_ON=y to preserve previous behavior.

Reviewer Vlastimil suggested replacing the current implementation with DEFINE_STATIC_KEY_MAYBE and disconnecting mempool debug from CONFIG_SLUB_DEBUG_ON, citing that it's not part of slab and should be decoupled.
reviews.raw_body:
The same would apply to the dmapool_debug [1]

https://lore.kernel.org/all/20260524034015.1830-1-lirongqing@baidu.com/

---

There's DEFINE_STATIC_KEY_MAYBE for this.

But I think nobody will care really. My objection was that this and
dmapool_debug was tied to CONFIG_SLUB_DEBUG_ON in the first place, despite
not being part of slab. I'd rather disconnect it completely.

If testing bots relied on this, we could give them heads up to start using
those boot options. I assume they already use some for stuff that has no
CONFIG_ enablement.

2026-05-27.patch_summary: This patch modifies the mempool debug setup to use a static key for boot-time debug enablement, preventing potential warnings about using static keys before jump_label_init(). The change allows existing users who relied on CONFIG_SLUB_DEBUG_ON=y to preserve their previous behavior by adding 'mempool_debug' to the command line. The patch also considers defaulting the static key to true under CONFIG_SLUB_DEBUG_ON=y to maintain consistency with the previous default behavior.

reviews.author: Li,Rongqing(ACG CCN)
reviews.summary: Reviewer Li,Rongqing pointed out that while the boot-time ordering issue is a generic problem, many architectures have already fixed it internally, and using early_param() to manage static keys is still accepted in the current kernel core. They argued that using __setup() would miss tracking for critical early-stage memory pools, making early_param() the most robust option.
reviews.raw_body:
Thanks for your review!

While this boot-time ordering used to be a generic issue, it seems many
architectures have already aligned or fixed this internally. For instance,

commit ca829e05d3d4 ("powerpc/64: Init jump labels before parse_early_param()")
and commit 6070970db9fe ("m68k: Initialize jump labels early during setup_arch()")
explicitly relocated jump_label_init() before the early parameter parsing.

Furthermore, leveraging early_param() to directly manage static keys is still
actively used and accepted in the current core kernel. Some examples include:

  - early_param("randomize_kstack_offset", early_randomize_kstack_offset);
  - early_param("threadirqs", setup_forced_irqthreads);

The primary reason for using early_param() here instead of __setup() is that
mempool allocations can happen extremely early during the boot phase. Moving
this to a later stage like __setup() would mean missing the tracking for the
most critical early-stage memory pools, which defeats the purpose of boot-time
debugging.

Therefore, I think using early_param() here is the most robust option to
ensure full coverage of mempool allocations.

What do you think?

Thanks

-Li

2026-05-28.patch_summary: This patch modifies the mempool debug setup to use a static key for boot-time debug enablement, preventing potential warnings about using static keys before jump_label_init(). The change allows existing users who relied on CONFIG_SLUB_DEBUG_ON=y to preserve their previous behavior by adding 'mempool_debug' to the command line. The patch also considers defaulting the static key to true under CONFIG_SLUB_DEBUG_ON=y to maintain consistency with the previous default behavior.
